lineage: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
The Emperor Gratian recalls the Orthodox Bishops, and expels the Heretics from the Churches. He takes Theodosius as his Colleague in the Empire.

ccel.title: NPNF2-02. Socrates and Sozomenus Ecclesiastical Histories

source.title: NPNF2-02. Socrates and Sozomenus Ecclesiastical Histories

title: The Emperor Gratian recalls the Orthodox Bishops, and expels the Heretics from the Churches. He takes Theodosius as his Colleague in the Empire.

source.title: NPNF2-02. Socrates and Sozomenus Ecclesiastical Histories

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Gratian being now in possession of the empire, together with Valentinian the younger, and condemning the cruel policy of his uncle Valens towards the [orthodox] Christians, recalled those whom he had sent into exile.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: He moreover enacted that persons of all sects, without distinction, might securely assemble together in their churches; and that only the Eunomians, Photinians, and Manichæans should be excluded from the churches.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Being also sensible of the languishing condition of the Roman empire, and of the growing power of the barbarians and perceiving that the state was in need of a brave and prudent man, he took Theodosius as his colleague in the sovereign power.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: This [Theodosius] was descended from a noble family in Spain, and had acquired so distinguished a celebrity for his prowess in the wars, that he was universally considered worthy of imperial dignity, even before Gratian’s election of him.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Having therefore proclaimed him emperor at Sirmium a city of Illyricum in the consulate of Ausonius and Olybrius, on the 16th of January, he divided with him the care of managing the war against the barbarians.
